notes: Reverse-translating ('the engineer whose buddhi has crossed the thicket of architecture-memes can evaluate new proposals against actual constraints without being captured by the next promised result') yields a stance compatible with all four traditions. The natural-not-cynical character is preserved (Shankara: nirveda arises naturally; Ramanuja: spontaneously). The scoped indifference is preserved (only to phala-promising chains, not to learning). The counter-example carves out genuine adoption requirements, preventing the misreading 'reflexive contrarianism'. No distortion. Pass.
